title: MCP: expose bug count by severity as a queryable tool
description: Our Java MCP server needs a new tool called getBugStats that returns the count of bugs grouped by severity. This will allow AI agents to query the live database state without going through the REST API.
acceptance_criteria: The MCP server exposes a tool named getBugStats
The tool accepts no input parameters
The tool returns a JSON object: { LOW: N, MEDIUM: N, HIGH: N, CRITICAL: N, total: N }
The tool queries the SQLite database directly using JDBC
The tool is correctly listed in the tools/list MCP response
A second tool getSummary is also added, returning overall platform stats: total tasks, open bugs, active flags
technical_notes: This work happens in the separate MCP server module (not in the Spring Boot app). The MCP server communicates over stdio using JSON-RPC 2.0. Add the new tool definitions to the tools/list handler and implement the tools/call handler for both new tools.
